topic.prompt_en: Describe something you genuinely enjoy or strongly prefer.
topic.learning_goal: Learn to express preference with reasons, contrast, and a natural personal example.

angles.title: State the preference clearly
content: Say what you prefer and, if useful, what you prefer it over.
A small contrast makes the answer easier to follow.

angles.title: Give practical reasons
content: Explain why the preference fits your personality or routine.

angles.title: Add one example
content: Use a daily or weekly example instead of a big story.
Preferences are often best explained through small patterns.

angles.title: Close with self-understanding
content: Show what the preference says about you.
This turns a simple opinion into a fuller answer.

sequence.target: Say what you prefer and set up a light contrast.
Explain why it works for you and show one real situation.
End with what the preference reflects about your nature or routine.

high_score_tips: Strong preference answers sound better when the contrast is clear but not aggressive.
Use preference verbs naturally: prefer, tend to choose, feel more comfortable with.
Do not try to defend your preference like an argument essay.
A quiet personal insight often gives the best ending.

content.why_it_hurts: The answer sounds too thin for a full 1-2 minute response.
content.fix: Add one practical reason and one personality-based reason.

content.why_it_hurts: The answer can sound extreme or immature.
content.fix: Use softer contrast such as it suits me better or I tend to feel more comfortable with.

content.why_it_hurts: The preference stays abstract and generic.
content.fix: Mention one context where your preference shows up clearly.

language.reason: Prefer already expresses comparison by itself.
Suits me better is a natural spoken phrase for personal preference.

core.example: I prefer smaller cafes to crowded chain stores.
I feel more focused in a quiet environment.
It is easier for me to stay focused in calmer places.
I get distracted in crowded places.
The atmosphere affects my mood more than I expected.
I can concentrate better in a quieter setting.

extended.note: A natural word for calm social settings or plans.
Useful when noisy places drain your attention.
Very natural spoken alternative to prefer.
Good for quiet or simple preferences.
Useful when explaining why you avoid noisy or crowded settings.
Strong flexible phrase for preference answers.

core.example: I tend to choose smaller cafes when I need to focus.
I feel more comfortable with quieter spaces.
I cannot focus when there is too much going on around me.
Low-key places suit me better than loud ones.
Quiet spaces leave room to think.
If I stay too long in noisy places, I feel drained afterward.

extended.phrase: not against it, just not my first choice
extended.meaning_zh: 不是反对，只是不是我的首选
extended.example: I am not against busy cafes, but they are not my first choice.

extended.phrase: a better fit for my personality
extended.note: Strong closing phrase for preference answers.
extended.example: That kind of place is simply a better fit for my personality.

extended.note: Good for calm environments or simple activities.
extended.example: Quieter spaces help me stay present.

extended.note: Useful for stronger Band 8 explanations of preference.
extended.example: I prefer calmer places because they help me avoid sensory overload.

framework.goal: Express preference with contrast, reasons, and one realistic example.

moves: Set up a gentle contrast if helpful.
Give two reasons why it works for you.
Add one specific context where the preference shows clearly.
Explain what the preference reflects about you.

delivery_markers: That works better for me because ...
I think it is simply a better fit for me.

samples.answer:
One preference I can describe quite clearly is that I prefer small, quiet cafes to large, noisy ones. I do not dislike busy places completely, but they usually make it harder for me to focus.

What I like about smaller cafes is the atmosphere. They are usually calmer, less crowded, and easier to sit in for an hour or two. If I want to read, write, or think about work, that kind of space helps me concentrate. In a loud cafe, I often notice too many conversations and movements around me.

For example, when I need to plan something or finish a few tasks, I almost always choose a quieter place. It suits me better because I like environments that feel simple and calm. I think that says a lot about my personality.
highlights: Links preference to concentration and personality

body: What I like about it is ...
If I want to ..., that kind of space ...

upgrades.improved: I prefer small, quiet cafes to large, noisy ones.
upgrades.why: The contrast immediately makes the answer clearer.

upgrades.improved: It suits me better because I like simple and calm environments.
upgrades.why: This sounds more natural and more personal.

samples.answer:
A preference I have noticed more clearly as I have grown older is that I strongly prefer low-key cafes to loud, crowded ones, especially when I am alone. It is not that I never enjoy busy social spaces, but they do not usually work well for me when I need to think.

The main reason is probably mental rather than practical. In quieter places, I can stay focused and feel more present. In louder environments, I get distracted easily and sometimes feel socially drained even if I am not actually talking to anyone. A smaller cafe with gentle background noise is a much better fit for me.

For instance, when I need to read or organize my week, I almost automatically choose a calm place with fewer people. That preference has taught me that I am someone who values space and clarity more than stimulation.
highlights: Balanced contrast with stronger emotional explanation

opening: A preference I have noticed more clearly is ...
It is not that I never enjoy ..., but ...
body: For instance, when I need to ...
closing: That preference has taught me that ...

upgrades.improved: Louder environments can leave me socially drained.
upgrades.why: This is more precise and natural.
upgrades.note: Useful when crowds affect your energy.

upgrades.improved: A calmer place is a much better fit for me.
upgrades.why: Better fit sounds more personal and balanced.
upgrades.note: A reliable phrase for preference answers.

samples.answer:
One preference that consistently shapes my daily choices is my tendency to choose quieter, more low-key spaces over loud and crowded ones. I do not mean that I reject busy places in principle; they are simply more stimulating than I usually want, especially when I am trying to think clearly.

What I value in a quieter environment is the sense of mental space it creates. A calm room, a smaller cafe, or even a less crowded corner of a public place leaves room for my attention to settle. By contrast, very noisy environments can feel mildly overstimulating, and after a while I notice that I become less present and more internally scattered.

That preference has become clearer to me over time because I now recognize it as part of how I function best. It is less about taste in the superficial sense and more about choosing conditions that help me stay focused, grounded, and emotionally balanced.
highlights: Band 8 answer explains preference as self-knowledge
Uses overstimulating and mentally space in a natural way

opening: I do not mean that I reject ... in principle ...
body: What I value in a quieter environment is ...
closing: It is less about taste ... and more about ...

upgrades.improved: I tend to choose quieter, more low-key spaces over loud and crowded ones.
upgrades.why: This sounds more natural and more flexible than a blunt like statement.
upgrades.note: Tend to choose is a very useful spoken pattern.

upgrades.improved: They create the conditions that help me stay focused and grounded.
upgrades.why: The explanation feels more mature and precise.
upgrades.note: Strong final insight for preference topics.

practice.task: Describe one thing you strongly prefer and explain why it suits you better than the alternative.
checklist: State the preference and a light contrast.
Give two reasons it works for you.
Add one real context and one reflective ending.
self_prompts: What do you usually choose instead?
Why does it work better for you?
What does that preference say about you?
